summary: Russia has redeployed a Bastion coastal missile system to Kursk region, dramatically shortening strike times on Kyiv. Ukraine continues high‑intensity strikes on Russian logistical targets, notably oil terminals and a naval base in the Black Sea. Drone activity remains intense on both sides, with Shahed attacks over central Ukraine and Russian UAV strikes on Sumy and Luhansk.
key_points: Russia moved a Bastion coastal missile complex with four launchers to Kursk region, enabling Onyx and Zircon missiles to reach Kyiv in seconds (Source: @eRadarrua/93149).
Ukrainian forces struck the Russian oil terminal “Sheshharis” on the Black Sea, igniting a fire at a 75 million‑ton capacity facility (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian drones hit the Russian frigate Admiral Essen and a 1239‑class missile boat near Novorossiysk naval base (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).

Shahed‑type UAVs were observed over Vyshorod, Vynohradiv, and Shepetivka, indicating continued Russian strike waves toward central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910, @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66916).
Russian UAVs attacked a funeral convoy in Sumy, causing at least one serious casualty (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).
Ukrainian attacks damaged transport facilities at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, destroying eight staff buses (Source: @tass_agency/377230).
High‑altitude reconnaissance UAVs increased activity over the Black Sea and near Crimea, with Orion drones operating close to the peninsula (Source: @eRadarrua/93118).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, targeting civilian infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62503, @kpszsu/62495).

sections.executive_summary: Russia’s relocation of a Bastion coastal missile system to Kursk now allows near‑instant strikes on Kyiv, raising strategic risk. Ukraine is intensifying its offensive, hitting Russian oil infrastructure, naval assets, and logistics with drones and missiles. Both sides sustain heavy UAV activity, with Shahed attacks over central Ukraine and Russian drones striking Sumy and Luhansk, indicating a volatile, high‑intensity conflict.

points: Ukrainian drones struck the Sheshharis oil terminal on the Black Sea, igniting a fire at a facility capable of handling up to 75 million tonnes of oil annually, degrading Russia’s fuel export capacity (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).

A coordinated strike by Ukrainian UAVs damaged the Admiral Essen frigate and a 1239‑class missile boat near the Novorossiysk naval base, reducing Russian surface‑naval capabilities in the Black Sea (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).

Ukrainian forces attacked transport infrastructure at Zaporizhzhia NPP, destroying eight staff buses and disrupting personnel movement, reflecting continued pressure on Russian‑held nuclear facilities (Source: @tass_agency/377230).

Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, targeting power and industrial sites, demonstrating expanded use of precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/62503, @kpszsu/62495).
Ukrainian intelligence reported high‑resolution reconnaissance UAVs operating over the Black Sea, supporting strike planning against Russian maritime assets (Source: @eRadarrua/93118).
Ukrainian forces continue building fortified defensive positions in Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk, aiming to blunt Russian advances in the north and east (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).

Ukrainian command noted increased Shahed‑type UAV incursions over Vyshorod, Vynohradiv, and Shepetivka, prompting heightened air‑defence alerts in central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910, @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66916).
Ukrainian drones targeted a convoy in Sumy, causing civilian casualties and highlighting the threat to rear‑area movements (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).

Russia redeployed a Bastion coastal missile system with four launchers to Kursk region, enabling Onyx and Zircon missiles to strike Kyiv within seconds, drastically reducing warning time (Source: @eRadarrua/93149).

Russian Shahed‑type UAVs conducted multiple sorties over central Ukrainian oblasts, including Vyshorod, Vynohradiv, and Shepetivka, sustaining pressure on Ukrainian air‑defences (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910, @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66916).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, focusing on civilian infrastructure and logistics (Source: @kpszsu/62503, @kpszsu/62495).
Russian UAVs attacked a funeral convoy in Sumy, injuring at least one person, demonstrating continued strike capability in northern Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).
Russian forces reported the downing of Ukrainian drones over Luhansk, resulting in two civilian injuries in the city centre (Source: @tass_agency/377236).

Russian reconnaissance UAVs increased activity over the Black Sea and near Crimea, with Orion drones operating close to the peninsula, suggesting preparation for strike missions (Source: @eRadarrua/93118).
Russian forces suffered losses of naval assets, including the Admiral Essen frigate and a missile boat, after Ukrainian drone attacks, reducing Black Sea fleet strength (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).
Russian authorities announced plans to host foreign journalists in LNR after the college attack, indicating a propaganda response to Ukrainian strikes (Source: @tass_agency/377238).
Ukrainian drones hit the Sheshharis oil terminal in the Black Sea, a key logistical hub for Russian forces in the south‑east (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian Shahed UAVs were observed over Vyshorod and Shepetivka, threatening supply routes in the central‑eastern axis (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910, @mon1tor_ua/66916).
Ukrainian tactical bombings targeted Zaporizhzhia transport facilities, disrupting Russian logistics in the eastern industrial belt (Source: @tass_agency/377230).
Russian guided aerial bomb strikes on Donetsk oblast aimed at power and industrial sites, attempting to degrade Ukrainian defensive capacity (Source: @kpszsu/62495).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs over the Black Sea support targeting of Russian maritime supply lines feeding the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93118).
Russian forces reported downing Ukrainian UAVs in the Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk regions, indicating contested airspace over the eastern heartland (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian fortified positions built in Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk aim to halt Russian advances from the north and east (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).
Russian Shahed attacks continue over central Ukraine, stretching eastern air‑defence resources (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910).
Russian Shahed UAVs flew over Vyshorod and Vynohradiv, indicating north‑west penetration toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910, @mon1tor_ua/66911).
Ukrainian fortified engineering works in Kharkiv north region aim to blunt Russian thrusts after recent assaults (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).
Russian UAVs reported over Sumy, including an attack on a funeral convoy causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).
Ukrainian reconnaissance drones observed near Chernihiv and Sumy, monitoring Russian movements (Source: @kpszsu/62487, @kpszsu/62511).
Russian tactical bombings targeted Zaporizhzhia, but northern units reported interceptions, showing active air‑defence (Source: @kpszsu/62503).
Ukrainian forces reported Shahed incursions over central‑northern oblasts, stretching Kyiv’s air‑defence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910).
Russian forces claimed downing of Ukrainian UAVs over the Bryansk region, indicating cross‑border engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian engineering units continue building fortifications in the north to protect supply lines to Kyiv (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).
Ukrainian drones struck the Sheshharis oil terminal on the Black Sea, a critical southern logistics node for Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian Shahed UAVs operated over Vynohradiv and Shepetivka, threatening southern supply corridors (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66911).
Ukrainian tactical bombings hit transport infrastructure at Zaporizhzhia NPP, affecting southern energy logistics (Source: @tass_agency/377230).
Russian guided aerial bombs were launched on Zaporizhzhia oblast, targeting civilian and military infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62503).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs over the Black Sea support targeting of Russian naval assets, including the Admiral Essen (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).
Russian Orion UAVs near Crimea suggest preparation for strikes against Ukrainian southern coastal positions (Source: @eRadarrua/93118).
Ukrainian fortified positions in Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk aim to secure southern advance routes (Source: @DeepStateUA/23529).
Russian Shahed attacks continue over central‑southern oblasts, testing Ukrainian air‑defences (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66911).

Russian Shahed‑type UAVs conducted multiple sorties over central Ukraine, including Vyshorod, Vynohradiv, and Shepetivka, challenging Ukrainian air‑defence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910, @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66916).
Ukrainian drones successfully struck the Admiral Essen frigate and a 1239‑class missile boat near Novorossiysk, degrading Russian naval air‑defence (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, indicating intensified use of precision strike assets (Source: @kpszsu/62503, @kpszsu/62495).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs increased activity over the Black Sea and near Crimea, supporting strike planning (Source: @eRadarrua/93118).
Russian UAVs attacked a funeral convoy in Sumy, demonstrating capability to strike ground targets with drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).
Ukrainian forces reported Shahed incursions over central Ukraine, stretching air‑defence resources (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910).
Russian forces claimed downing of Ukrainian UAVs over LNR and Bryansk, showing contested airspace (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian tactical bombings hit transport facilities at Zaporizhzhia NPP, disrupting Russian logistical air‑bridge (Source: @tass_agency/377230).

key_developments: Russia redeployed a Bastion coastal missile system with four launchers to Kursk, cutting Kyiv strike times to seconds (Source: @eRadarrua/93149).
Ukrainian drones ignited a fire at the Sheshharis oil terminal, a 75 million‑ton capacity Black Sea facility (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Ukrainian UAVs damaged the Russian frigate Admiral Essen and a missile boat near Novorossiysk, reducing Black Sea fleet capability (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).

Shahed‑type UAVs were observed over Vyshorod, Vynohradiv, and Shepetivka, indicating sustained Russian strike pressure on central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66910, @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66916).
Russian drones attacked a funeral convoy in Sumy, causing at least one serious casualty (Source: @UkraineNow/81399).
Ukrainian attacks damaged transport infrastructure at Zaporizhzhia NPP, destroying eight staff buses (Source: @tass_agency/377230).
High‑altitude Orion UAVs operated near Crimea, suggesting imminent Russian strike missions in the Black Sea (Source: @eRadarrua/93118).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia’s relocation of the Bastion missile system dramatically raises the strategic threat to Kyiv, enabling near‑instant hypersonic strikes. Ukrainian UAV and missile operations are increasingly effective, targeting Russian naval assets and critical logistics hubs, but face growing Russian Shahed drone pressure across central and northern Ukraine. Both sides are intensifying air‑defence engagements, indicating a high‑risk environment with rapid escalation potential.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are monitoring the Bastion deployment, with NATO likely to increase air‑defence support for Ukraine. Russia may seek to leverage the new missile capability for political pressure, while Ukrainian forces are bolstering fortifications in the north and east to absorb continued drone attacks. Regional actors, including neighboring states, are on heightened alert for spill‑over incidents.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued tracking of Shahed UAV flight paths is essential to predict strike vectors toward Kyiv and supply corridors. Monitoring of Russian guided bomb sorties will inform Ukrainian civil‑defence readiness in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. Satellite and SIGINT should focus on the Bastion system’s operational status and potential launch patterns from Kursk.
